mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are positive in >60% of periods when FEDFUNDS increases.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 6.0%, it signals a deteriorating labor market and impending recession, causing investors to de-risk and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >55% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 6.0%, or the signal loses predictive power with Rank IC < 0.01 during high-unemployment periods.

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of rising real auto sales and declining federal funds rates indicates robust consumer spending supported by cheap credit, which directly boosts corporate revenues and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample tests, or forward returns are negative in >60% of periods where both conditions align.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and supply-side capacity utilization, which triggers short-term momentum buying and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The signal reverses direction during post-shock recovery periods, producing negative 5-day returns in >50% of cases, or Rank IC falls below 0.015 over rolling 1-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth directly correlates with expanding corporate profit pools, creating a persistent upward drift in equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in >55% of quarters following positive GDPC1 revisions, or the correlation coefficient drops below 0.10 in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 255.0, it indicates entrenched inflation that forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity multiples and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in >60% of periods where CPIAUCSL remains above 255.0, or the threshold effect vanishes in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and new housing starts signals a synchronized expansion in labor demand and construction activity, boosting consumer confidence and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns in >50% of synchronized growth periods, or the interaction term predictive IC is indistinguishable from zero over 2-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in industrial production combined with rising unemployment indicates a broad-based economic contraction, prompting defensive portfolio shifts and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >55% of periods where both indicators deteriorate simultaneously, or the combined signal Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.
